Title: Overexpression of ZmPCK2, a phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase gene from maize confers enhanced tolerance to water deficit stress in rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Endogenous rice PCK genes OsPCK1 and OsPCK2 are down-regulated by PEG-induced water-deficit in leaves, contrasting with the stress-inducible ZmPCK2, suggesting functional divergence between maize and rice PCK isoforms in drought response.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Maize ZmPCK2 encodes a phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase whose expression in maize leaves is strongly induced by PEG-simulated water-deficit and ABA treatments.
2. Overexpression of ZmPCK2 in rice (indica 9311) increases water-deficit tolerance, as indicated by higher biomass, chlorophyll content, F_v/F_m, and relative water content and lower water loss under PEG-induced stress.
3. ZmPCK2 overexpression promotes stomatal closure, decreases stomatal conductance and leaf malate levels, thereby enhancing water-saving capacity.
4. Under water-deficit conditions, ZmPCK2 overexpression up-regulates ABA biosynthesis genes (OsNCED4, OsNCED5) and ABA-responsive genes (OsRAB16A, OsRAB16D), increases ABA-induced stomatal closure and ABA sensitivity during germination.
5. ZmPCK2 overexpression enhances seed germination under mannitol-induced osmotic stress, accompanied by reduced malate and increased total free amino acids in seeds.
6. ZmPCK2 overexpression reduces ROS (O2−, H2O2) accumulation and MDA content and increases SOD, POD and CAT activities under water-deficit stress, conferring improved ROS-scavenging capacity and reduced oxidative damage.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of ZmPCK2 in rice enhances tolerance to water-deficit stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: ZmPCK2 overexpression enhances PCK enzymatic activity, lowering malate levels associated with stomatal closure and improved water retention; under water-deficit stress it increases transcript levels of ABA biosynthesis genes OsNCED4 and OsNCED5 and ABA-responsive genes OsRAB16A and OsRAB16D, leading to higher ABA responsiveness and ABA-induced stomatal closure; it also elevates activities of antioxidant enzymes SOD, POD and CAT, thereby reducing ROS (O2−, H2O2) and lipid peroxidation (MDA).
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA biosynthesis and signaling pathway (via up-regulation of OsNCED4, OsNCED5, OsRAB16A, OsRAB16D), stomatal closure pathway via malate metabolism, and antioxidant/ROS-scavenging pathways.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Gluconeogenesis and malate metabolism (PCK-catalyzed decarboxylation of oxaloacetate to phosphoenolpyruvate affecting malate/OAA/PEP balance).
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: ZmPCK2 overexpression -->(activation)--> OsNCED4 and OsNCED5 (ABA biosynthesis genes) under water-deficit stress; ZmPCK2 overexpression -->(activation)--> OsRAB16A and OsRAB16D (ABA-responsive genes) under water-deficit stress, leading to enhanced ABA signaling and stomatal closure.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In maize, ZmPCK2 is strongly induced in leaves by PEG6000 (water deficit) and ABA treatments. In ZmPCK2-overexpressing rice, the transgene is expressed in multiple tissues with highest expression in leaves at the productive stage.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Water-deficit stress simulated by 20% PEG6000 in nutrient solution; osmotic stress induced by 200 mM mannitol during seed germination; ABA treatments (50 µM for stomatal assays; 5 µM during germination).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (water-deficit/drought), stomatal regulation, ABA signaling, ROS-scavenging and metabolic regulation in cereal crops.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (water-deficit/drought tolerance; osmotic stress tolerance during seed germination).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Leaf malate levels after 3 d water-deficit were reduced from 1.34 µmol g^-1 FW in NT to 0.61 and 0.97 µmol g^-1 FW in OE-5 and OE-12, respectively; ZmPCK2 overexpression lines showed significantly higher fresh weight, chlorophyll content, F_v/F_m, RWC, antioxidant enzyme activities (SOD, POD, CAT), and germination rates under mannitol stress, and significantly lower water loss rates, ROS (O2−, H2O2) and MDA levels than NT (exact percentages given in figures).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: In transgenic rice, ZmPCK2 overexpression confers more green leaves, higher fresh weight, chlorophyll content and F_v/F_m under PEG-induced water deficit; increases stomatal closure and reduces stomatal conductance and leaf water loss, resulting in higher relative water content; lowers malate levels in leaves and seeds while increasing total free amino acids during germination; enhances seed germination under mannitol-induced water-deficit stress; up-regulates ABA biosynthesis genes (OsNCED4, OsNCED5) and ABA-responsive genes (OsRAB16A, OsRAB16D) under water deficit; increases ABA-induced stomatal closure and ABA hypersensitivity at germination; reduces O2− and H2O2 accumulation and MDA content under water-deficit stress; and increases SOD, POD and CAT activities, indicating a stronger antioxidant system.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Maize inbred line B73 seedlings; transgenic rice (indica cv. 9311) overexpressing ZmPCK2 (OE-5, OE-12 T3 lines); non-transgenic (NT) and empty vector control rice plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of rice with ZmPCK2 overexpression construct; RT-PCR and qRT-PCR for transgene and endogenous gene expression; PEG6000 treatment to simulate water-deficit stress; ABA treatments; mannitol-induced osmotic stress during germination; measurements of fresh weight, chlorophyll content, F_v/F_m, stomatal conductance, leaf relative water content and water loss; scanning electron microscopy of stomata; enzymatic malate quantification; total free amino acid assay; NBT and DAB staining for O2− and H2O2; MDA content assay; SOD, POD and CAT activity assays; germination assays under ABA or mannitol.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation via transgenic overexpression of ZmPCK2 in rice followed by physiological, biochemical and gene-expression analyses under PEG-induced water deficit, ABA treatment and mannitol-induced osmotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Full-length ZmPCK2 coding sequence from maize was cloned into pCAMBIA1300 under the maize ubiquitin promoter and introduced into rice (indica 9311) via Agrobacterium-mediated transformation to generate overexpression lines.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: ZmPCK2 is a promising candidate gene for engineering water-saving and water-deficit-tolerant rice and possibly other crops; its overexpression improves drought-related traits, including stomatal water-saving capacity, seed germination under water-deficit conditions and ROS-scavenging, indicating potential utility in breeding or biotechnological development of drought-resilient cultivars.
